claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines four valid scopes: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: Schema-level scope enumeration confirms all four scope values are defined

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION fields.evidence explicitly mentions scope is project | external | user | unknown; project facts require project-scoped evidence
evidence.why: Evidence schema definition documents scope field values and project-scoped requirement

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker, verifier with writes_code=false and scope-aware prompts
evidence.why: Agent protocols enforce non-mutation and distinguish project from external sources

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies by explicit scope, URL detection, then source keyword patterns including project_code, project_test, external_web
evidence.why: Scope classification logic distinguishes project from external evidence via multiple heuristics

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope checks VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES.has; evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator validates non-URL path presence
evidence.why: Project evidence validation functions enforce scope declaration and concrete locator requirements

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues returns PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blockers
evidence.why: Readiness contract enforces project-scoped evidence for existing-project facts

evidence.summary: isNonMissingStatusItem filters status notes like no missing data identified; actionableMissingItems excludes them
evidence.why: Missing array processing logic distinguishes status notes from actual blockers

evidence.summary: Test: external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts, with externalEvidence mock and PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker
evidence.why: Test validates external evidence cannot substitute for project facts

evidence.summary: Test: project evidence requires concrete locator, with locatorlessProjectEvidence mock and PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: Test validates project evidence must have concrete path

evidence.summary: Test: external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence, with mislabeledExternalEvidence mock and PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: Test validates URL evidence cannot pass as project evidence

evidence.summary: Test: evidence records must declare valid scope, with scopeLessEvidence mock and EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: Test validates scope declaration is mandatory

evidence.summary: Test: non-missing status notes do not block readiness, with missing=['No missing data identified'] passing prd_ready=true
evidence.why: Test validates status-only notes are not treated as blockers

evidence.summary: Test validates DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines three agents and inspectEvidenceAgreement detects conflicts
evidence.why: Test validates evidence agent protocol definitions and conflict detection

risks: Evidence scope classification could fail if agents provide malformed scope or source values
Project evidence validation could be bypassed if path validation logic is incomplete

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all explorer evidence. All four evidence scopes (project, external, user, unknown) are defined in VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES, enforced through evidenceRecordScope classification, validated by projectEvidenceBlockingIssues in readiness contract, and covered by comprehensive tests. Schema requires scope field; agent protocols define scope-aware prompts; tests validate external evidence cannot substitute project facts, project evidence requires concrete locators, URLs cannot pass as project evidence, scope must be declared, and status-only notes do not block. No conflicts found.
